commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces five empty Discord component interaction catch blocks in reply, acknowledge, and follow-up paths with `console.warn` diagnostics.

PR surface: Source +10. Total +10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main has empty catch blocks around the affected Discord reply, acknowledge, and follow-up calls, and a rejecting interaction would currently resolve without logging there. I did not run a live Discord failure scenario.

target.title: fix(discord): log warnings instead of silently swallowing component interaction errors
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Two Discord component interaction files use empty `catch {}` blocks that silently swallow all errors:

- `agent-components-reply.ts:10` — `replySilently()` drops all reply failures
- `exec-approvals.ts:88,100,113,125` — interaction reply/acknowledge/followUp failures silently dropped

When Discord API returns errors (rate limits, expired interactions, permission issues), neither the user nor the operator sees any indication. This is **D5** dimension: error visibility.

## Changes

- `agent-components-reply.ts`: `catch {}` → `catch (err) { console.warn(...) }`
- `exec-approvals.ts`: all 4 `catch {}` → `catch (err) { console.warn(...) }` with context-specific labels

**Scope**: 5 empty catches replaced with diagnostic logging. No behavior change — errors are still swallowed gracefully, just now with a console warning for operators.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted | dimension: D5 error visibility
